element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic for destroying flying enemies; introduced immediately with no tutorial
Paired weapon system; reticle shows landing zone; core mechanic from beginning
Initial threat; game dynamically escalates to harder enemy types once player demonstrates mastery
Requires blaster targeting; rewards dual-weapon strategy
Dynamically introduced when player excels; skill-based difficulty scaling
Destruction resets enemy difficulty back to easier types; strategic choice point
Boss encounter; launches endless projectiles and 'Zakatos' spheres; requires targeting core or four blaster receptacles
Secret discovery mechanic; reticle flashes red when overhead; rewards exploration
Extra life reward; semi-random placement encourages thorough area coverage
Forests, roads, rivers, mechanical structures; visual variety; Nazca line decorations (condor designs)

typical_relief_triggers: Destroying Zolback radars to reset enemy difficulty
Collecting Special Flags for extra lives
Completing an area checkpoint (70% rule allows mid-area respawn)
typical_tension_triggers: Endless stream of projectiles and Zakatos during boss phases
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Dynamic difficulty scaling tied to player performance; tension rises as player demonstrates mastery; punctuated by boss encounters at specific progression points; relief available through strategic radar destruction

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Reticle flashes red when positioned over hidden Sol Citadels; encourages systematic bombing of ground areas
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden towers (Sol Citadels) require bombing specific unmarked ground locations; discovered via reticle feedback rather than visual cues

difficulty_curve.early_game: Welcoming to new players; basic enemy types; dual-weapon system teaches itself through immediate feedback
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Gradual escalation to advanced enemy types triggered by player skill; Zolback radars introduce strategic choice (accept harder difficulty or reset)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained advanced enemies; boss encounters with Andor Genesis mothership; 16-area loop structure allows indefinite progression
notable_spikes: First Andor Genesis mothership encounter (endless projectiles + Zakatos)
Transition to advanced enemy types (dynamically triggered by combat success)
Loop cycles back to area 1 after area 16 (difficulty maintained)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game monitors player performance destroying specific enemy types and automatically replaces them with advanced variants; players can manually reset to easier enemies by destroying Zolback radars
Each area contains both airborne targets (zapper targets) and ground installations (blaster targets); design rewards players who master both weapons
Sol Citadels exist in unmarked ground locations; reticle provides only feedback (red flash) rather than explicit markers; encourages thorough area bombing
Dying approximately 70% through an area respawns player at beginning of next area; reduces frustration on long areas while maintaining progression stakes
Andor Genesis mothership appears at certain progression points; player must either destroy four blaster receptacles OR the central core; launches infinite projectiles and explosive spheres
